title: globularia incanescens
intro: Globularia incanescens, also known as woolly globe-mallow, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its greyish-green foliage and clusters of blue or white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Globularia incanescens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Globularia incanescens can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.
